name: oh-my-issues
tagline: Clusters a GitHub issue backlog into architectural plan-master issues, closes duplicates with a redirect, and bundles fixes into one PR per
verdict: Well-scoped GitHub triage workflow using only the official gh CLI, no fetched-code execution or hidden instructions. Main risk: it closes and creates issues in bulk directly against a live repo (Modes 1-3) with no explicit user-confirmation gate before executing, and it never states gh/jq as required prerequisites.

highlights.summary: Bulk-closes issues and creates new master issues directly (e.g. 'Close every child', 'Verify end state') with no instruction to confirm with the user before running these live-repo mutations.
Extensively depends on 'gh' CLI (authenticated) and 'jq' but never states these as explicit prerequisites anywhere in the file.
No version field or change-log signal in frontmatter or body, so consumers cannot tell if/when the skill was last updated.
